name: Webprocess Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof that the Self-Driven Project Artefact Step/ProcessRecipe/ArtefactRef contract (#1070) and its driving loop are conformant. The runtime (the meta-schema registries, default-recipe resolution, the dependency-graph frontier, and the tolerance→autonomy throttle) is relocated to Frontier UI (#2294, WE holds zero executable); its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788/#1790) driving the WE-owned webprocess vector suite (#2295: dependency-graph frontier, run completion, throttled ceiling, the OPEN autonomy ladder, and the trust-crossing seam guard) against the FUI binding. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside a cross-origin iframe.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof that the Self-Driven Project Artefact Step / ProcessRecipe / ArtefactRef contract (#1070) and its driving loop are conformant. The runtime — the meta-schema registries, default-recipe resolution, the dependency-graph frontier, and the tolerance→autonomy throttle — is relocated to Frontier UI (#2294; WE holds zero executable, #1282). Its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 / #1790), which drives the WE webprocess vector suite (#2295) against the FUI binding.

Verified contract

WE owns only the build-agnostic vectors and the contract; the driving-loop impl is FUI's, the runner is plateau's. The vector suite asserts: the default seam ships the L0–L5 autonomy ladder + the webprocess/default recipe; a malformed Step/ProcessRecipe is rejected at the seam; a dangling dependency edge is caught; the dependency-graph frontier and run completion resolve correctly; effectiveCeiling throttles DOWN per low-tolerance dimension (never grants, floored at L0), with recipe ceiling overrides applied before the throttle; and the autonomy ladder is OPEN to new rungs. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside an iframe — the cross-origin boundary (#1788 ratified (b)) that reaches the shared, neutral runner with no implementer→runner edge. WE's own backlog is one dogfooded recipe — a consumer of the standard, never the spec. A green result proves conformance independent of how the runtime is built (#899).
